_doc: Category-compatibility table for diagnosis_score_v1_1.
Used ONLY by evaluator scoring (category_match_score). Does NOT rewrite
ground-truth category labels.
Each entry is keyed by the GROUND-TRUTH (post-alias) category. The 'partial'
list names predicted categories that score 0.5 instead of 0.0 when a diagnosis
lands on a related-but-not-identical bucket.
Exact match still scores 1.0 in every case; partial match scores 0.5; anything
not listed scores 0.0.
Discipline:
- Partial pairs must be defensible — i.e. an engineer reading the wrong
category would still investigate the right area.
- test_assertion is INTENTIONALLY paired with workflow gates
(lint_failure, formatting_failure, github_actions_config) because those
gates fail via assertion-style checks that look like test failures.
- compile_error <-> type_error are paired because rustc / tsc error texts
can read as either depending on the surface form.
- dependency_install <-> network_or_flaky are paired because failed installs
can be either a config issue or a network blip without distinguishing
evidence.
- permission_or_secret <-> github_actions_config are paired because token /
workflow-permission misconfigurations land in either bucket.
- docker_build <-> compile_error are paired (a Dockerfile compile-stage
failure looks like both).
- 'other' has NO partial entries on purpose — it must not silently absorb
real misclassifications.
